# Core library
add_library(kalshi_core STATIC
    core/error.cpp
    core/rate_limit.cpp
    core/retry.cpp
)
target_compile_features(kalshi_core PUBLIC cxx_std_23)
target_include_directories(kalshi_core PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    # version.hpp is generated by configure_file() in the top-level
    # CMakeLists.txt at ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/include/kalshi/version.hpp
    # (relative to the kalshi-cpp project, not the consumer's top level).
    # Must match the configure_file destination — using CMAKE_BINARY_DIR
    # here breaks FetchContent consumers because that resolves to the
    # consumer's top-level build dir, not the kalshi-cpp subproject dir.
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)

# Auth library (RSA-PSS signing)
add_library(kalshi_auth STATIC
    auth/signer.cpp
)
target_link_libraries(kalshi_auth PUBLIC kalshi_core OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
target_include_directories(kalshi_auth PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)

# HTTP client library
add_library(kalshi_http STATIC
    http/client.cpp
)
target_link_libraries(kalshi_http PUBLIC kalshi_core kalshi_auth CURL::libcurl)
target_include_directories(kalshi_http PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)

# Models library
add_library(kalshi_models STATIC
    models/market.cpp
    models/order.cpp
)
target_link_libraries(kalshi_models PUBLIC kalshi_core)
target_include_directories(kalshi_models PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)

# WebSocket library
add_library(kalshi_ws STATIC
    ws/websocket.cpp
)
if(NOT WIN32)
    set(_KALSHI_WS_TARGET PkgConfig::WEBSOCKETS)
else()
    set(_KALSHI_WS_TARGET websockets_shared)
    # libwebsockets pulls in <windows.h> on Windows, which by default
    # #defines ``max`` and ``min`` as function-like macros. Glaze's
    # headers use ``std::numeric_limits<T>::max()`` extensively (e.g.
    # ``glaze/core/buffer_traits.hpp``, ``glaze/util/fast_float.hpp``,
    # ``glaze/json/read.hpp``); once those macros are live, every such
    # call expands to garbage and the build dies with a 100+ error
    # cascade (C2589 / C3878 / C2760) — first triggered on PR #19
    # Windows CI. NOMINMAX suppresses the macro definitions so
    # ``std::numeric_limits<T>::max()`` parses cleanly. The
    # include-order fix in ws/websocket.cpp (Glaze shim before
    # ``<libwebsockets.h>``) is the primary defense; this is
    # belt-and-brace for any future Windows header that might land
    # above the Glaze include.
    target_compile_definitions(kalshi_ws PRIVATE NOMINMAX)
endif()
# NOTE: Glaze is pulled in via include-path only (matching how
# nlohmann was integrated pre-migration). This sidesteps CMake's
# "INTERFACE target not in export set" error that fires if we link
# glaze::glaze directly onto these installable static libs. Glaze is
# header-only, so the include path is the only thing the TU needs;
# downstream consumers (kalshi-trader) never see these headers
# because the include path is BUILD_INTERFACE-only and the symbols
# never escape the .cpp (the shim structs + glz::meta are in an
# anonymous namespace inside the TU).
target_link_libraries(kalshi_ws PUBLIC kalshi_core kalshi_auth kalshi_models ${_KALSHI_WS_TARGET})
target_include_directories(kalshi_ws PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)
target_include_directories(kalshi_ws PRIVATE
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${glaze_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
)

# API client library (full REST endpoint coverage)
add_library(kalshi_api STATIC
    api/client.cpp
)
target_link_libraries(kalshi_api PUBLIC kalshi_core kalshi_http kalshi_models)
target_include_directories(kalshi_api PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)
target_include_directories(kalshi_api PRIVATE
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${glaze_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
)

# Main SDK library (combines all)
add_library(kalshi INTERFACE)
target_link_libraries(kalshi INTERFACE kalshi_core kalshi_auth kalshi_http kalshi_models kalshi_ws kalshi_api)
